system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        DICGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        "(k|epsilon)"   1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

scale   0.001;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    ( -50  25  25 )   // 0
    ( -50  25 -25 )   // 1
    ( -50 -25 -25 )   // 2
    ( -50 -25  25 )   // 3

    (   0  25  25 )   // 4
    (   0  25 -25 )   // 5
    (   0 -25 -25 )   // 6
    (   0 -25  25 )   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    (  25   0  25 )   // 8
    (  25   0 -25 )   // 9
    (  25 -50 -25 )   // 10
    (  25 -50  25 )   // 11

    // Block 2 - outlet straight duct
    (  25  -75  25 )   // 12
    (  25  -75 -25 )   // 13
    (  25 -125 -25 )   // 14
    (  25 -125  25 )   // 15

    ( -500  -75  25 )   // 16
    ( -500  -75 -25 )   // 17
    ( -500 -125 -25 )   // 18
    ( -500 -125  25 )   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 11 10 6 3 2 9 8)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1a: Transition from bend to outlet
    hex (11 10 12 13 3 14 15 2)
    (10 10 10)
    simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet straight section
    hex (12 13 16 17 15 14 19 18) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    upperWall

        type wall;
        faces

            // Block 0 top
            (0 4 5 1)
            // Block 1 top
            (7 8 9 6)
            // Block 1a top
            (3 11 10 2)
            // Block 2 top
            (12 16 17 13)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 bottom
            (3 7 6 2)
            // Block 1 bottom
            (3 2 10 11)
            // Block 1a bottom
            (15 3 2 14)
            // Block 2 bottom
            (15 14 18 19)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 front
            (0 3 7 4)
            // Block 1 front
            (7 11 3 8)
            // Block 1a front
            (11 15 3 12)
            // Block 2 front
            (12 15 19 16)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 back
            (1 5 6 2)
            // Block 1 back
            (6 2 9 8)
            // Block 1a back
            (2 14 15 10)
            // Block 2 back
            (13 14 18 17)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: `log.blockMesh` — Block 1 has "inward-pointing faces"

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (7 11 10 6 3 2 9 8) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(11 10 9 2) 4(7 11 2 3) 4(3 2 9 8)
```

**Diagnosis:**

The issue is with Block 1's hex definition: `hex (7 11 10 6 3 2 9 8)`. Let me examine the vertex coordinates:

- Vertex 7: (0, -25, 25)
- Vertex 11: (25, -50, 25)
- Vertex 10: (25, -50, -25)
- Vertex 6: (0, -25, -25)
- Vertex 3: (-50, -25, 25)
- Vertex 2: (-50, -25, -25)
- Vertex 9: (25, 0, -25)
- Vertex 8: (25, 0, 25)

The problem is that vertices 7→11→10→6 lie in the same plane (z varies, but the face formed doesn't follow the right-hand rule properly). More critically, vertex 9 and 8 are at z=-25 and z=25 respectively, while vertices 7 and 6 are also at those heights — meaning vertices 9 and 8 are coplanar with vertices 10 and 11, creating a degenerate/inverted hex.

**Root Cause:**

The bend geometry topology is fundamentally flawed. The vertices were placed such that the "bend" block doesn't form a proper convex hexahedron. The vertex positions create a non-convex shape where the block twists in a way that makes one or more faces have inward-pointing normals.

**Proposed Fix:**

Redesign the vertex layout and block connectivity to match the actual squareBend tutorial geometry. The bend should curve from the inlet duct (along +x) turning toward the outlet duct (along -x through y=-75). Here's the corrected approach:

1. **Reorganize vertices** to properly define a bend. A suggested vertex layout:
   - Keep vertices 0-7 for the inlet block (unchanged)
   - Redefine bend vertices: place the bend corner at the origin (0,0,±25) and extend along the arc
   - Place outlet vertices far in the -x direction (e.g., around x=-500, y≈-75)

2. **Corrected block definitions** should produce positive-volume hexahedra. Specifically, Block 1 should connect the top of the inlet duct to the top of the outlet duct through the bend. The face normals must all point outward.

3. **Key principle**: For each hex block `(v0 v1 v2 v3 v4 v5 v6 v7)`, the face `(v0 v1 v2 v3)` and `(v4 v5 v6 v7)` must have opposite normals, and the side faces must be consistent. Use the right-hand rule consistently.

Since manual reconstruction is error-prone, I strongly recommend **directly copying the vertex coordinates, block definitions, and boundary patches from the official OpenFOAM squareBend tutorial** (`$FOAM_TUTORIALS/compressible/rhoSimpleFoam/squareBend`), then only adjusting the vertex positions to match the user-provided coordinates if they differ significantly.

---

## Error 2: `log.rhoSimpleFoam` — Cannot find "points"

**Error Message:**
```
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh"
```

**Diagnosis:** This is a cascading error. Since `blockMesh` failed, no mesh was generated. **Fix the blockMesh error first.**

---

## Summary of Required Changes

1. **`system/blockMeshDict`**: Completely revise the vertex coordinates and block connectivity for Blocks 1 and 2 (and potentially Block 0) to create a geometrically valid bend. Each hex block must have positive volume with all face normals pointing outward. The current vertex positions for the bend region create degenerate/inverted hexahedra.

2. **All `0/` field files** (`U`, `k`, `p`, `alpha`, `epsilon`, `nut`): Ensure boundary patch names exactly match those defined in `blockMeshDict` (`inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`). Previous attempts showed some files still had incorrect patch names.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace entire blockMeshDict content with corrected version that fixes inward-pointing faces; Redesign vertex coordinates and block connectivity to create a valid 3D square bend geometry with positive-volume hex blocks; Use corrected vertex ordering that follows right-hand rule for all faces; Define 4 blocks: inlet straight section, bend section, transition section, outlet straight section with proper grading; Ensure all boundary patches (inlet, outlet, upperWall, lowerWall, leftWall, rightWall) reference valid faces with outward normals')]
